response.justification: While the candidate meets the educational baseline (Bachelor's + 8+ years experience) and demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise, this role requires specialized hardware architecture knowledge fundamentally absent from their background. Their career is rooted in full-stack web, mobile, and cloud infrastructure development—not the SoC design, GPU architecture analysis, and deep neural network optimization that are core to this Principal Performance Architect role. Recent AI infrastructure work is too application-focused to offset the critical gap in hardware-level systems expertise.
strengths: 18+ years of professional software engineering experience with proven ability to optimize complex systems
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq demonstrates willingness to engage with emerging AI technologies
Strong background in performance optimization and low-latency systems (high-performance databases, microservices)
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, Verilog/VHDL, or hardware description languages required for core responsibilities
No background in GPU architecture, CUDA, or deep learning workload profiling and optimization
Career trajectory entirely in application software (web, mobile, platforms) rather than systems architecture or hardware design

key_factors.factor: Deep Learning Optimization & GPU Architecture Experience
